prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial begins with an animated scene of an old, dusty attic-like room. The room is drawn in thin black outlines, with a tall arched window in the center background, wooden floorboards, cobwebs in several corners, and stored objects such as a large chest or trunk on the right and a tub or container with bottles on the left. A small lit area appears on the floor near the center, where tiny dark marks or insect-like shapes are visible, though the exact objects are not fully clear.

In the next sampled frames, the same animated attic remains on screen, but the scene becomes more strongly tinted blue. The arched window glows brightly, and the patch of light on the floor becomes more noticeable. The small dark shapes on the floor remain in that illuminated area. Cobwebs and stored objects continue to frame the space, emphasizing an old or neglected interior.

There is then a large gap in the sampled frames, so the intervening action is not visible. By the next sampled frame, the commercial has shifted from animation to live-action footage. A hand is holding an aerosol can in an indoor setting. The can has a blue cap and a dark label with the word “Raid” visible. The background is out of focus, with warm interior lighting and what appears to be a table lamp or shade behind it.

The final frame presents a clear product shot: a can of “Raid Flying Insect Killer” standing upright in the foreground. The can has a blue cap, a black central label with large yellow “Raid” lettering, and smaller text reading “Flying Insect Killer.” A small insect graphic is visible near the lower part of the label. To the right of the can, the text “Johnson Wax” appears on screen. The background remains softly blurred, keeping attention on the product.
